cards.frontJa:
Complete B (present habit). A: Do you walk to work?
B: Yes, ___
cards.backEn: I walk to work every day.

cards.frontJa:
Past: Last night I ___ (not finish) my homework.
Choose the best sentence.
cards.backEn: I didn't finish my homework last night.

cards.frontJa:
Future: Choose the best plan sentence.
We're ___ dinner at my place on Saturday.
cards.backEn: We're going to have dinner at my place on Saturday.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 毎朝シャワーを浴びます。
cards.backEn: I take a shower every morning.

cards.frontJa:
Choose the best past sentence.
She ___ in London for two years. (live — past)
cards.backEn: She lived in London for two years.

cards.frontJa: Present vs future: I ___ my aunt tomorrow afternoon. (visit — plan with "going to")
cards.backEn: I'm going to visit my aunt tomorrow afternoon.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 昨日は忙しかった。
cards.backEn: I was busy yesterday.

cards.frontJa:
Pick the natural present simple line.
Water ___ at 100°C at sea level.
cards.backEn: Water boils at 100°C at sea level.

cards.frontJa: Complete: By 2030, many cities ___ more electric buses. (use — future with will)
cards.backEn: By 2030, many cities will use more electric buses.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 今週末、映画を見るつもりです。
cards.backEn: I'm going to watch a movie this weekend.

cards.frontJa:
Past question: ___ you ___ the email yesterday? (send)
Answer as a full question.
cards.backEn: Did you send the email yesterday?

cards.frontJa: Present continuous for arrangement: I ___ my dentist on Tuesday at 3. (see)
cards.backEn: I'm seeing my dentist on Tuesday at 3.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼はもう昼ご飯を食べました。
cards.backEn: He has already eaten lunch.

cards.frontJa:
Simple future (will) for instant decision: A: I'm cold.
B: I ___ you my jacket.
cards.backEn: I'll lend you my jacket.

cards.frontJa: Past habit (used to): When I was a child, I ___ afraid of dogs.
cards.backEn: When I was a child, I was afraid of dogs.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 来月から新しい仕事を始めます。
cards.backEn: I'm starting a new job next month.

cards.frontJa:
Choose the best sentence.
They ___ to Japan three times so far. (travel — present perfect)
cards.backEn: They have traveled to Japan three times so far.

cards.frontJa: Future with time clause: After I ___ dinner, I'll call you. (finish — present after "After I")
cards.backEn: After I finish dinner, I'll call you.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女は昨夜遅くまで働いていました。
cards.backEn: She was working late last night.

cards.frontJa: Mixed: I ___ English since 2020, and I still practice every week. (study)
cards.backEn: I have studied English since 2020, and I still practice every week.

cards.frontJa:
Two-step practice (English only).

Complete both steps — you will submit after step 2.
cards.backEn: I'm planning to see my mother next Friday evening.

steps.prompt:
Step 1 — translate into natural English:
来週の金曜日、母に会う予定です。
steps.canonical: I'm going to see my mother next Friday.

steps.prompt:
Step 2 — add a time of day ("evening") and keep the sentence natural.
Write your final English sentence.
steps.canonical: I'm planning to see my mother next Friday evening.

cards.frontJa:
Experience question about a place (Osaka).
___ you ever been to Osaka?
cards.backEn: Have you ever been to Osaka?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 寿司を食べたことがありません。
cards.backEn: I have never tried sushi.

cards.frontJa: Experience with "already": She ___ her ticket. (buy — present perfect)
cards.backEn: She has already bought her ticket.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その映画をもう見ましたか？
cards.backEn: Have you seen that movie yet?

cards.frontJa: Negative experience: I ___ that app before. (not use — present perfect)
cards.backEn: I haven't used that app before.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は海外に行ったことがあります。
cards.backEn: He has been abroad before.

cards.frontJa: Question: How many countries ___ you ___? (visit — present perfect)
cards.backEn: How many countries have you visited?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私は一度もスキーをしたことがありません。
cards.backEn: I have never been skiing.

cards.frontJa: Since + point in time: We ___ here since Monday. (wait — present perfect continuous acceptable; use present perfect)
cards.backEn: We have waited here since Monday.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女はその本を読み終えています。
cards.backEn: She has finished reading that book.

cards.frontJa: Have you ever ___ Japanese curry at home? (make)
cards.backEn: Have you ever made Japanese curry at home?

cards.frontJa: Translate: まだ昼食を食べていません。
cards.backEn: I haven't eaten lunch yet.

cards.frontJa: Experience + superlative: This is the best ramen I ___ . (ever / taste)
cards.backEn: This is the best ramen I have ever tasted.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼らは富士山に登ったことがあります。
cards.backEn: They have climbed Mount Fuji before.

cards.frontJa:
Short answer style: A: Have you tried matcha?
B: Yes, ___ . (I / try / it)
cards.backEn: Yes, I have tried it.

cards.frontJa: Translate: そのニュースを聞いたことがありますか？
cards.backEn: Have you heard that news before?

cards.frontJa: Present perfect result: I ___ my keys. I can't open the door. (lose)
cards.backEn: I have lost my keys. I can't open the door.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私は東京に住んだことがあります。
cards.backEn: I have lived in Tokyo before.

cards.frontJa: Ever + present perfect: ___ you ever ___ a speech in public? (give)
cards.backEn: Have you ever given a speech in public?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼はまだ返事を書いていません。
cards.backEn: He hasn't written a reply yet.

cards.frontJa: Experience paragraph starter: I've ___ a lot of interesting people at work. (meet)
cards.backEn: I've met a lot of interesting people at work.

decks.titleJa: つなぎ言葉（and / but / so / because）

cards.frontJa:
Choose the connector.
It was raining, ___ we still went hiking.
cards.backEn: It was raining, but we still went hiking.

cards.frontJa:
Choose the connector.
I was tired, ___ I went to bed early.
cards.backEn: I was tired, so I went to bed early.

cards.frontJa:
Choose the connector.
She stayed home ___ she felt sick.
cards.backEn: She stayed home because she felt sick.

cards.frontJa: Translate: コーヒーが欲しいです。とても眠いです。
cards.backEn: I want coffee because I'm very sleepy.

cards.frontJa:
Choose the connector.
I bought milk ___ eggs.
cards.backEn: I bought milk and eggs.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 安かったので、二つ買いました。
cards.backEn: It was cheap, so I bought two.

cards.frontJa:
Choose the best sentence.
He likes sports, ___ he doesn't like running.
cards.backEn: He likes sports, but he doesn't like running.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 遅刻しました。バスが来なかったので。
cards.backEn: I was late because the bus didn't come.

cards.frontJa:
Reason vs result: pick the right connector (not "so" here).
I left early ___ there was heavy traffic.
cards.backEn: I left early because there was heavy traffic.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は勉強しました。そして試験に合格しました。
cards.backEn: He studied, and he passed the exam.

cards.frontJa:
Fix the logic: hunger is the reason, eating is the result.
I was hungry, ___ I ate a sandwich.
cards.backEn: I was hungry, so I ate a sandwich.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 雨が降っていたが、試合は続いた。
cards.backEn: It was raining, but the game continued.

cards.frontJa:
Choose the connector.
She speaks quietly, ___ everyone listens carefully.
cards.backEn: She speaks quietly, so everyone listens carefully.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は忙しいので、今夜は来られません。
cards.backEn: He's busy, so he can't come tonight.

cards.frontJa:
Add a reason with the right connector.
I like this cafe ___ the staff are friendly.
cards.backEn: I like this cafe because the staff are friendly.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私は泳げます。しかし、深い水は怖いです。
cards.backEn: I can swim, but I'm afraid of deep water.

cards.frontJa: Choose: I forgot my umbrella, ___ I bought a cheap one at the store.
cards.backEn: I forgot my umbrella, so I bought a cheap one at the store.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女は疲れていたので、早く寝ました。
cards.backEn: She was tired, so she went to bed early.

cards.frontJa:
Polite short reply: A: Want to grab lunch?
B: I'd love to, ___ I'm in a meeting until 1.
cards.backEn: I'd love to, but I'm in a meeting until 1.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は親切だ。そしてとても頼りになる。
cards.backEn: He is kind, and he is very reliable.

cards.frontJa: Because-clause first: ___ I left my charger at home, my phone died.
cards.backEn: Because I left my charger at home, my phone died.

decks.titleJa: 質問の形（How long… / How often…）

cards.frontJa:
They live in Sapporo. Ask how long:
___ have you lived in Sapporo?
cards.backEn: How long have you lived in Sapporo?

cards.frontJa:
They go to the gym on Mondays and Thursdays. Ask how often:
___ do you go to the gym?
cards.backEn: How often do you go to the gym?

cards.frontJa: Translate: どのくらいの間、英語を勉強していますか？
cards.backEn: How long have you been studying English?

cards.frontJa:
Natural follow-up: A: I started yoga last month.
B: ___ do you practice each week?
cards.backEn: How often do you practice each week?

cards.frontJa: Translate: ここに来てからどのくらいですか？
cards.backEn: How long have you been here?

cards.frontJa:
Choose the best question.
___ does it take to get to the airport by train?
cards.backEn: How long does it take to get to the airport by train?

cards.frontJa: Translate: どのくらいの頻度でニュースを見ますか？
cards.backEn: How often do you watch the news?

cards.frontJa: How long + present perfect continuous: ___ you ___ this book? (read — question)
cards.backEn: How long have you been reading this book?

cards.frontJa: Translate: その仕事を始めてからどれくらいですか？
cards.backEn: How long have you had that job?

cards.frontJa: How often + simple present: ___ you ___ your grandparents? (visit)
cards.backEn: How often do you visit your grandparents?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 会議はどのくらい続きますか？
cards.backEn: How long will the meeting last?

cards.frontJa:
They look tired. Ask politely:
___ did you sleep?
cards.backEn: How long did you sleep?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 週に何回走りますか？
cards.backEn: How many times a week do you run?

cards.frontJa: How long for future wait: ___ will we have to wait?
cards.backEn: How long will we have to wait?

cards.frontJa: Translate: どのくらいの間、その痛みがありますか？
cards.backEn: How long have you had that pain?

cards.frontJa: Ask about frequency of being late: ___ are you late to class?
cards.backEn: How often are you late to class?

cards.frontJa: Translate: どのくらいの間、彼を知っていますか？
cards.backEn: How long have you known him?

cards.frontJa: Choose: ___ do you clean your room — every day or once a week?
cards.backEn: How often do you clean your room — every day or once a week?

cards.frontJa: Translate: その列に並んでからどれくらいですか？
cards.backEn: How long have you been standing in this line?

cards.frontJa:
Small talk: A: I commute by bike.
B: ___ is your commute?
cards.backEn: How long is your commute?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 月に何回映画を見ますか？
cards.backEn: How many times a month do you watch movies?

cards.frontJa:
She always helps new coworkers. Complete the sentence.
She is very ___. (helpful)
cards.backEn: She is very helpful.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼はとても社交的で、すぐに友達を作ります。
cards.backEn: He is very outgoing and makes friends easily.

cards.frontJa:
Describe his appearance in one sentence.
He ___ tall and has short dark hair and glasses.
cards.backEn: He is tall and has short dark hair and glasses.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女は静かだけど、とても頭がいい。
cards.backEn: She is quiet, but very smart.

cards.frontJa: Polite description for a teacher: strict ___ fair
cards.backEn: Our teacher is strict but fair.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼はいつも笑顔で、ポジティブな人だ。
cards.backEn: He always smiles; he is a positive person.

cards.frontJa: Use "looks": She ___ tired today.
cards.backEn: She looks tired today.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女は背が高くて、モデルのようだ。
cards.backEn: She is tall and looks like a model.

cards.frontJa:
Describe a friend (loyal + funny).
My friend is ___ and funny.
cards.backEn: My friend is loyal and funny.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は少し恥ずかしがり屋だが、優しい。
cards.backEn: He is a little shy, but kind.

cards.frontJa: Adjective order (simple): a ___ ___ coat (small / warm)
cards.backEn: a small warm coat

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女は声が大きくて、リーダー向きだ。
cards.backEn: She has a loud voice; she is good at leading.

cards.frontJa: Personality vs mood: He isn't rude; he ___ just stressed today.
cards.backEn: He isn't rude; he is just stressed today.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は真剣な顔つきをしているが、冗談が好きだ。
cards.backEn: He looks serious, but he likes jokes.

cards.frontJa:
A: Your sister is really creative.
B: I agree — she ___ really creative.
cards.backEn: I agree — she is really creative.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女はとても忍耐強い母親だ。
cards.backEn: She is a very patient mother.

cards.frontJa:
Someone you admire is hard-working.
My boss is extremely ___.
cards.backEn: My boss is extremely hard-working.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は細身で、走るのが速い。
cards.backEn: He is slim and runs fast.

cards.frontJa: Fill in the blank: He ___ a bit nervous before presentations.
cards.backEn: He seems a bit nervous before presentations.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その店員さんは丁寧で親切だった。
cards.backEn: The clerk was polite and kind.

cards.frontJa:
Compare two friends (one calm, one energetic).
One friend is calm, ___ the other is energetic.
cards.backEn: One friend is calm, while the other is energetic.

cards.frontJa:
At a café: you want a medium hot latte to go.
___ get a medium hot latte to go, please?
cards.backEn: Can I get a medium hot latte to go, please?

cards.frontJa:
A: What would you like to drink?
B: ___ (water / please)
cards.backEn: Water, please.

cards.frontJa: Translate: お会計をお願いします。
cards.backEn: The check, please.

cards.frontJa:
Suggest Saturday afternoon for a museum visit.
___ you free Saturday afternoon to go to the museum?
cards.backEn: Are you free Saturday afternoon to go to the museum?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 今夜、一緒に夕食を食べませんか？
cards.backEn: Would you like to have dinner together tonight?

cards.frontJa:
Phone call: you answer and give your name (Ken).
Hello, ___ Ken speaking.
cards.backEn: Hello, this is Ken speaking.

cards.frontJa: Translate: すみません、メニューを見せてください。
cards.backEn: Excuse me, could I see the menu, please?

cards.frontJa:
At a store: ask about a larger size for this shirt.
___ you have this shirt in a larger size?
cards.backEn: Do you have this shirt in a larger size?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 予約はできますか？
cards.backEn: Can I make a reservation?

cards.frontJa:
Train station: ask about the express to Osaka.
___ platform is the express train to Osaka?
cards.backEn: Which platform is the express train to Osaka?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 荷物を預けられますか？
cards.backEn: Can I leave my luggage here?

cards.frontJa:
Apartment issue: tell the manager the AC is not working.
The ___ in my apartment isn't working.
cards.backEn: The air conditioner in my apartment isn't working.

cards.frontJa: Translate: すみません、道に迷いました。
cards.backEn: Excuse me, I'm lost.

cards.frontJa:
Invite someone to a movie on Friday evening.
___ you like to see a movie on Friday evening?
cards.backEn: Would you like to see a movie on Friday evening?

cards.frontJa: Translate: テーブルは二人です。
cards.backEn: A table for two, please.

cards.frontJa:
Doctor visit: mild symptom + how long.
I've had ___ for two days.
cards.backEn: I've had a sore throat for two days.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もう少し待っていただけますか？
cards.backEn: Could you wait a little longer, please?

cards.frontJa:
Hotel: request a wake-up call at 6:30 a.m.
___ I have a wake-up call at 6:30 a.m., please?
cards.backEn: Could I have a wake-up call at 6:30 a.m., please?

cards.frontJa: Translate: この席、空いていますか？
cards.backEn: Is this seat taken?

cards.frontJa:
Friend is running late. Text them:
___ will you be?
cards.backEn: How long will you be?

cards.frontJa:
The shoes hurt your feet; you want a refund.
I'd ___ to return these shoes for a refund, please.
cards.backEn: I'd like to return these shoes for a refund, please.

cards.frontJa:
Your friend is stressed before an exam.
You ___ take a short break and breathe.
cards.backEn: You should take a short break and breathe.

cards.frontJa: Translate: ここでは写真を撮ってはいけません。
cards.backEn: You must not take photos here.

cards.frontJa: Ability: She ___ three languages fluently. (speak — can)
cards.backEn: She can speak three languages fluently.

cards.frontJa: Translate: もっと水を飲むべきです。
cards.backEn: You should drink more water.

cards.frontJa: Strong obligation: Visitors ___ their IDs at the gate.
cards.backEn: Visitors must show their IDs at the gate.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 明日までにこれを終えなければなりません。
cards.backEn: I must finish this by tomorrow.

cards.frontJa:
Polite request: open the window (can/could).
___ you open the window, please?
cards.backEn: Could you open the window, please?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は泳げますが、深いプールは苦手です。
cards.backEn: He can swim, but he doesn't like deep pools.

cards.frontJa:
Soft advice: you look sick.
You ___ see a doctor.
cards.backEn: You should see a doctor.

cards.frontJa: Translate: ここでタバコを吸ってはいけません。
cards.backEn: You can't smoke here.

cards.frontJa: Prohibition sign meaning: Employees ___ food in the lab.
cards.backEn: Employees must not eat food in the lab.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 早めに出発した方がいいですよ。
cards.backEn: You should leave earlier.

cards.frontJa: Ability question: ___ you ___ me tomorrow? (help)
cards.backEn: Can you help me tomorrow?

cards.frontJa: Translate: その書類に署名しなければなりません。
cards.backEn: You must sign that document.

cards.frontJa: Should vs must (nuance): For health, you ___ brush your teeth twice a day. (strong habit rule)
cards.backEn: You should brush your teeth twice a day.

cards.frontJa: Translate: そのドアは開かないかもしれません。
cards.backEn: That door might not open.

cards.frontJa: Permission: ___ I sit here?
cards.backEn: May I sit here?

cards.frontJa: Translate: 試験では電卓を使ってはいけません。
cards.backEn: You must not use a calculator on the exam.

cards.frontJa:
It's raining hard.
You ___ take an umbrella.
cards.backEn: You should take an umbrella.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女はピアノを弾けます。
cards.backEn: She can play the piano.

cards.frontJa: Obligation past: I ___ leave early yesterday. (have to)
cards.backEn: I had to leave early yesterday.

cards.frontJa: Opinion + reason: I like this neighborhood ___ it's quiet.
cards.backEn: I like this neighborhood because it's quiet.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 遅れたのは、電車が止まったからです。
cards.backEn: I'm late because the train stopped.

cards.frontJa:
You can't go to a party (work is the reason).
I can't come ___ I have to work late.
cards.backEn: I can't come because I have to work late.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は疲れているので、家で休みたいと言った。
cards.backEn: He said he wants to rest at home because he's tired.

cards.frontJa: Reason with "so": It was noisy, ___ I closed the window.
cards.backEn: It was noisy, so I closed the window.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私がその仕事を引き受けたのは、経験を積みたいからです。
cards.backEn: I took the job because I want to gain experience.

cards.frontJa: Because + noun phrase: I'm learning English ___ my career.
cards.backEn: I'm learning English for my career.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女はお金を節約したいので、外食を減らした。
cards.backEn: She cut down on eating out because she wants to save money.

cards.frontJa:
Answer why: A: Why did you choose this hotel?
B: ___ (good reviews)
cards.backEn: Because it had good reviews.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 雨が降っていたので、試合は中止になった。
cards.backEn: The game was canceled because it was raining.

cards.frontJa: Two reasons: I'm tired ___ I didn't sleep well ___ I worked a double shift.
cards.backEn: I'm tired because I didn't sleep well and I worked a double shift.

cards.frontJa: Translate: そのレストランが好きなのは、料理が新鮮だからです。
cards.backEn: I like that restaurant because the food is fresh.

cards.frontJa: Explain preference: I prefer tea ___ it helps me relax.
cards.backEn: I prefer tea because it helps me relax.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は忙しかったので、メールにすぐ返信できなかった。
cards.backEn: He couldn't reply to the email quickly because he was busy.

cards.frontJa: Because-clause first: ___ the traffic was bad, we missed the start.
cards.backEn: Because the traffic was bad, we missed the start.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私が早退したのは、子供が病気だったからです。
cards.backEn: I left early because my child was sick.

cards.frontJa:
You missed the bus. Give the reason in a short sentence.
I'm late ___ I missed the bus.
cards.backEn: I'm late because I missed the bus.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は練習したので、上手になった。
cards.backEn: He improved because he practiced.

cards.frontJa: Reason with "since": Since it's your first day, ___ . (be gentle — use "we" or "I'll")
cards.backEn: Since it's your first day, I'll show you around.

cards.frontJa: Translate: そのアプリが便利なのは、無料だからです。
cards.backEn: That app is convenient because it's free.

cards.frontJa: Polite: I can't eat nuts ___ I'm allergic.
cards.backEn: I can't eat nuts because I'm allergic.

decks.titleEn: Basic opinions (I think / I don't think)

cards.frontJa:
Soft opinion: this movie is too long.
I think ___ movie is too long.
cards.backEn: I think this movie is too long.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私はそれが良いアイデアだとは思いません。
cards.backEn: I don't think that's a good idea.

cards.frontJa:
Agree politely: A: The test was fair.
B: ___
cards.backEn: I think so, too.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼はもっと休むべきだと思います。
cards.backEn: I think he should rest more.

cards.frontJa:
Disagree gently: A: We should leave now.
B: ___ (not ready)
cards.backEn: I don't think we're ready to leave yet.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その計画は現実的ではないと思います。
cards.backEn: I don't think that plan is realistic.

cards.frontJa: Opinion + reason: ___ online classes are convenient because you save commute time.
cards.backEn: I think online classes are convenient because you save commute time.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私には難しすぎると思います。
cards.backEn: I think it's too difficult for me.

cards.frontJa: Hedging: ___ it might rain later, but I'm not sure.
cards.backEn: I think it might rain later, but I'm not sure.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女の方が正しいと思います。
cards.backEn: I think she's right.

cards.frontJa:
I don't think + positive verb (expect a negative meaning).
I ___ he will come.
cards.backEn: I don't think he will come.

cards.frontJa: Translate: それは公平だと思います。
cards.backEn: I think that's fair.

cards.frontJa:
Commuting by bike is healthy. Give your view.
___ my opinion, commuting by bike is healthy.
cards.backEn: In my opinion, commuting by bike is healthy.

cards.frontJa: Translate: それは必要ないと思います。
cards.backEn: I don't think it's necessary.

cards.frontJa: Compare opinions: ___ Team A will win, but it's hard to predict.
cards.backEn: I think Team A will win, but it's hard to predict.

cards.frontJa: Translate: この本は面白いと思います。
cards.backEn: I think this book is interesting.

cards.frontJa:
Politely disagree: A: This is the best ramen in town.
B: ___ (good / not sure best)
cards.backEn: I think it's good, but I'm not sure it's the best.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は来ないと思います。
cards.backEn: I don't think he'll come.

cards.frontJa: I feel like: ___ we should wait five more minutes.
cards.backEn: I feel like we should wait five more minutes.

cards.frontJa: Translate: その色の方が似合うと思います。
cards.backEn: I think that color suits you better.

cards.frontJa: Stronger (still polite): I'm not an expert, but ___ this answer is wrong.
cards.backEn: I'm not an expert, but I think this answer is wrong.

cards.frontJa: Habit: I ___ eat breakfast at home on weekdays. (frequency adverb: usually / often / rarely)
cards.backEn: I usually eat breakfast at home on weekdays.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私はたいてい自宅で仕事をします。
cards.backEn: I usually work from home.

cards.frontJa:
Short answer to "How often do you read?"
About ___ a week.
cards.backEn: About twice a week.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼はめったに遅刻しません。
cards.backEn: He is rarely late.

cards.frontJa: Sentence with "often": She ___ forgets her keys.
cards.backEn: She often forgets her keys.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私は時々寿司を食べます。
cards.backEn: I sometimes eat sushi.

cards.frontJa: Never + present simple: I ___ sugar in my coffee.
cards.backEn: I never put sugar in my coffee.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼はいつも朝早く起きます。
cards.backEn: He always wakes up early in the morning.

cards.frontJa: Hardly ever: I ___ stay out past midnight.
cards.backEn: I hardly ever stay out past midnight.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 週末はよくハイキングに行きます。
cards.backEn: I often go hiking on weekends.

cards.frontJa:
Usually vs used to (careful): I ___ drink soda every day, but now I don't.
Choose the best opening.
cards.backEn: I used to drink soda every day, but now I don't.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私はめったにテレビを見ません。
cards.backEn: I rarely watch TV.

cards.frontJa: Adverb placement: I have been to that museum ___ . (only once)
cards.backEn: I have been to that museum only once.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼女はたいてい徒歩で通勤します。
cards.backEn: She usually commutes on foot.

cards.frontJa:
Short answer to "How often…?" (gym, swimming, etc.)
___ or four times a month.
cards.backEn: Three or four times a month.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私は朝はいつもコーヒーを飲みます。
cards.backEn: I always drink coffee in the morning.

cards.frontJa: Frequency with "once": We meet ___ a month for lunch.
cards.backEn: We meet once a month for lunch.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 彼は時々仕事で出張します。
cards.backEn: He sometimes travels for work.

cards.frontJa: Seldom: I ___ eat fast food nowadays.
cards.backEn: I seldom eat fast food nowadays.

cards.frontJa: Translate: 私は週に三回ジムに行きます。
cards.backEn: I go to the gym three times a week.

cards.frontJa: Often + position: My phone ___ dies before bedtime.
cards.backEn: My phone often dies before bedtime.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder into one correct sentence:
want / I / to / go / to / the / store
cards.backEn: I want to go to the store.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
did / you / where / last / summer / go
cards.backEn: Where did you go last summer?

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
never / I / have / tried / sushi
cards.backEn: I have never tried sushi.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
because / tired / I / was / I / early / left
cards.backEn: I left early because I was tired.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
should / you / more / vegetables / eat
cards.backEn: You should eat more vegetables.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
me / can / you / help / with / this
cards.backEn: Can you help me with this?

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
think / I / this / is / too / expensive
cards.backEn: I think this is too expensive.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
often / how / do / you / exercise
cards.backEn: How often do you exercise?

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
has / she / lived / here / long / how
cards.backEn: How long has she lived here?

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
going / am / to / visit / I / my / friend
cards.backEn: I am going to visit my friend.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
the / check / please / may / have / I
cards.backEn: May I have the check, please?

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
usually / breakfast / I / at / 7 / have
cards.backEn: I usually have breakfast at 7.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
but / it / was / raining / we / still / went
cards.backEn: It was raining, but we still went.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
must / you / your / seatbelt / fasten
cards.backEn: You must fasten your seatbelt.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
so / I / bought / one / umbrella / I / forgot / my
cards.backEn: I forgot my umbrella, so I bought one.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
think / don't / I / that / is / safe
cards.backEn: I don't think that is safe.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
been / have / you / ever / to / Canada
cards.backEn: Have you ever been to Canada?

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
kind / very / she / is / and / patient
cards.backEn: She is very kind and patient.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
will / we / meet / at / the / station / tomorrow
cards.backEn: We will meet at the station tomorrow.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
because / stayed / she / home / felt / she / sick
cards.backEn: She stayed home because she felt sick.

cards.frontJa:
Reorder:
should / we / leave / now / I / think
cards.backEn: I think we should leave now.

cards.frontJa:
A: What are you doing this weekend?
B: ___ (visit a friend — natural)
cards.backEn: I'm going to visit my friend.

cards.frontJa:
A: How was the interview?
B: ___ (stressful / but okay — one sentence)
cards.backEn: It was stressful, but I think it went okay.

cards.frontJa:
A: Why are you late?
B: ___ (missed the bus)
cards.backEn: I'm late because I missed the bus.

cards.frontJa:
A: Can you help me move on Saturday?
B: ___ (sorry / work — polite decline + reason)
cards.backEn: I'm sorry, I can't because I have to work.

cards.frontJa:
A: How often do you study English?
B: ___
cards.backEn: I study English almost every day.

cards.frontJa:
A: You look tired.
B: ___ (didn't sleep well)
cards.backEn: I didn't sleep well last night.

cards.frontJa:
A: Should we take a taxi?
B: ___ (save money / take the train)
cards.backEn: We should take the train to save money.

cards.frontJa:
A: Have you been to Kyoto?
B: ___ (once / spring)
cards.backEn: I've been there once, in spring.

cards.frontJa:
A: I think this café is too loud.
B: ___ (agree / suggest leaving)
cards.backEn: I agree — maybe we should find another place.

cards.frontJa:
A: May I sit here?
B: ___ (yes / please)
cards.backEn: Yes, please do.

cards.frontJa:
A: What do you think of the new teacher?
B: ___ (strict / fair)
cards.backEn: She seems strict, but fair.

cards.frontJa:
A: I'm nervous about the presentation.
B: ___ (should / breathe — advice)
cards.backEn: You should take a deep breath and speak slowly.

cards.frontJa:
A: How long have you lived here?
B: ___ (two years)
cards.backEn: I've lived here for two years.

cards.frontJa:
A: Do you want dessert?
B: ___ (full / tea only)
cards.backEn: I'm full, so I'll just have tea.

cards.frontJa:
A: Can I get the bill?
B: ___ (sure / moment)
cards.backEn: Sure — I'll bring it in a moment.

cards.frontJa:
A: Why do you like this neighborhood?
B: ___ (quiet / close to park)
cards.backEn: Because it's quiet and close to a park.

cards.frontJa:
A: I don't think he'll come.
B: ___ (maybe / traffic)
cards.backEn: Maybe he's stuck in traffic.

cards.frontJa:
A: We should leave soon.
B: ___ (five more minutes — request)
cards.backEn: Can we wait five more minutes?

cards.frontJa:
A: Is this seat taken?
B: ___ (no / free)
cards.backEn: No, it's free.

cards.frontJa:
A: What would you like to drink?
B: ___ (hot green tea)
cards.backEn: Hot green tea, please.

cards.frontJa:
A: I'm sorry I'm late.
B: ___ (no problem / sit)
cards.backEn: No problem — please have a seat.

items.promptJa: 「I have never tried sushi.」に最も近い文は？
items.promptEn: Which sentence is closest to “I have never tried sushi.”?
options: I have tried sushi many times.
I don't like fish, so I never eat.

items.promptJa: 丁寧に断るのに自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which is a natural polite decline with a reason?
options: I'm sorry, I can't because I have to work late.

items.promptJa: 「How often do you exercise?」の意味に近いのは？
items.promptEn: Which question asks about frequency of exercise?

items.promptJa: 助言として自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which sentence gives natural advice before an exam?
options: You should take a short break and breathe.
You can to sleep in class.

items.promptJa: 理由をつなぐのに最も適切なのは？
items.promptEn: Which connector best expresses a reason?

items.promptJa: 「I don't think that's a good idea.」に近い意味は？
items.promptEn: Which sentence is closest to “I don't think that's a good idea.”?
options: I think that's a great idea.
I don't think that's a good idea.
I know that's a good idea.

items.promptJa: カフェで注文として自然なのは？
items.promptEn: Which is a natural café order?
options: Can I get a medium hot latte to go, please?
I want coffee medium hot go.

items.promptJa:
並べ替えの正しい文はどれ？
words: want / I / to / go / to / the / store
items.promptEn: Which sentence correctly orders: want / I / to / go / to / the / store?
options: I go want to the store to.
I want to go to the store.
To store the I want go to.
Want I to store go the to.

items.promptJa: 「How long have you lived here?」に対する自然な答えは？
items.promptEn: Which is a natural answer to “How long have you lived here?”?
options: I've lived here for two years.

items.promptJa:
会話の続きとして自然な B は？
A: What are you doing this weekend?
items.promptEn:
Which is a natural B line?
A: What are you doing this weekend?
options: I'm going to visit my friend.
